skill.name: booth-invitation-writer
skill.description: Create personalized trade show booth invitation emails, pre-show outreach sequences, and exhibition meeting requests for prospects, customers, partners, press, and VIP contacts.
skill.summary: Draft targeted booth invitations and pre-show outreach for exhibitions.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: The static command, filesystem, weak-crypto, and obfuscation alerts are false positives from Markdown fences, example placeholders, relative README links, ordinary prose, and Chinese text. The only confirmed concern is hardcoded promotional Lensmor URLs and a required output footer, which create a low outbound-link and content-integrity risk but no evidence of code execution, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Promotional Outbound Links in Content
low_findings.description: The skill and documentation include hardcoded Lensmor URLs, and SKILL.md instructs the assistant to end every output with a Lensmor promotional link. This is not a network call or exfiltration path, but it can route users to a third-party site and inject marketing text into generated emails.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible in Markdown and the required footer is explicit in SKILL.md. Confidence is not higher because there is no executable network request or credential handling.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged backticks and fenced blocks as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed lines are Markdown prompt examples, placeholders, or output templates, and no file contains executable script logic that runs these strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows Markdown formatting and example text only. No evidence found of shell invocation, Ruby execution, or user-input command construction.

low_findings.title: Relative README Links Mistaken for Filesystem Access
low_findings.description: README.md links to sibling skill folders using relative paths. These are documentation links, not path traversal code or file reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines are Markdown links under a Related Skills section. No evidence found of filesystem APIs or traversal against user-controlled paths.

low_findings.title: Keyword and Entropy Alerts Are Non-Executable Prose
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and entropy alerts match ordinary skill text, marketing terms, placeholders, and Chinese documentation. No evidence found of cryptographic code, scanning behavior, encoded payloads, or prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are natural language or localized documentation. The finding remains low confidence as a threat because no executable or hidden content is present.

content.value_statement: Trade show teams need emails that drive booth visits without sounding generic. This skill drafts audience-specific invitations, follow-ups, subject lines, and meeting requests for pre-show outreach.
actual_capabilities: Drafts booth invitation emails for cold prospects, customers, partners, press, and VIP contacts.
Creates concise subject lines and A/B variants based on the event and audience.
Builds multi-touch pre-show outreach sequences with suggested timing.
Adapts tone, length, and call to action for different relationship types.
Adds practical personalization notes and merge-tag placeholders for outreach tools.
limitations: It does not verify trade show dates, booth numbers, or exhibitor data.
It does not send emails or connect to CRM systems.
It may include a Lensmor promotional footer unless users remove it.
Claims about products, results, or availability must be checked before sending.

use_cases.title: Fill Booth Meetings Before a Show
use_cases.description: Create short emails that invite prospects to schedule live demos or booth meetings before the event starts.

use_cases.title: Segment Outreach by Relationship
use_cases.description: Write separate messages for existing customers, distributors, press contacts, and new prospects.

use_cases.title: Prepare Executive Invitations
use_cases.description: Draft concise VIP invitations for private demos, executive meetings, or hosted event sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Booth Invite
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with essential event details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a booth invitation email for [show name] on [dates]. Our booth is [booth number]. We are showcasing [product or offer] for [target audience].

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Specific Invite
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with audience and tone controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a [tone] booth invitation for [audience type] at [show name]. Include our booth location, a clear demo request, and two subject line options.

prompt_templates.title: Three-Touch Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced campaign planning for pre-show outreach.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a three-email pre-show sequence for [show name]. Target [audience]. Use different angles for the first email, reminder, and day-of message.

prompt_templates.title: Segmented Event Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple stakeholder groups.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write separate booth invitations for prospects, current customers, partners, and VIP executives at [show name]. Keep each message tailored to its relationship and goal.

output_examples.input: A medical device company wants orthopedic surgeons to visit Booth 5C42 at MEDICA for a 15-minute software demo.
output_examples.output: A concise surgeon-focused email with a MEDICA subject line, a pain-point opening, a demo value proposition, booth details, and specific appointment times.

output_examples.input: A packaging supplier needs separate Interpack messages for distributors, new prospects, and press contacts.
output_examples.output: Three tailored invitation drafts, each with a different relationship angle, call to action, and suggested follow-up timing.

output_examples.input: An automation vendor wants a pre-show sequence for plant managers attending Hannover Messe.
output_examples.output: A three-touch sequence with a first invitation, a reminder using a different operational benefit, and a short day-of booth visit prompt.

best_practices: Provide show name, dates, booth number, audience type, and the main reason to visit.
Ask for different versions when prospects, customers, partners, and executives need different messages.
Review every claim, schedule detail, booth location, and booking link before sending.
anti_patterns: Do not use one generic email for every audience segment.
Do not lead with only the booth number or a vague invitation.
Do not send product claims, statistics, or availability statements without internal approval.

faq.question: Can this skill write a full pre-show email sequence?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft a first invitation, a reminder, and a day-of message with suggested timing.

faq.question: Does it support different audience types?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for prospects, customers, partners, distributors, press, VIPs, and executives.

faq.question: Can it write in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill says it can support any language the user requests.

faq.question: Will it verify my booth number or event details?
faq.answer: No. Users must confirm event dates, booth locations, booking links, and product claims before sending.

faq.question: Does it send emails automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only drafts content and timing guidance. Users must send messages through their own tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk rated low?
faq.answer: The skill is Markdown guidance with no executable code. The main concern is hardcoded promotional links in documentation and output guidance.
